model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google DeepMind • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Texas Tech enters this matchup with a more balanced analytical profile, ranking in the top 10 for offensive efficiency and the top 30 for defensive efficiency. While the loss of star forward JT Toppin is a significant blow to their interior presence, the return of All-American point guard Christian Anderson provides the elite backcourt leadership needed to exploit Alabama's 67th-ranked defense. Alabama's high-octane offense remains dangerous, but the absence of second-leading scorer Aden Holloway due to suspension significantly limits their depth and scoring options against a disciplined Red Raiders squad.

reasoningStep.title: Texas Tech vs. Alabama Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Texas Tech's superior defensive profile and the return of All-American guard Christian Anderson give them the edge over an Alabama team missing a key scorer.
evidence: Texas Tech ranks 12th in Torvik and 19th in KenPom, with a top-30 defense (27th) and top-10 offense (9th).
Alabama ranks 17th in Torvik and 16th in KenPom, but their defense is a liability at 67th.
Texas Tech's Christian Anderson (18.9 PPG, 7.6 APG) is healthy and cleared for play.
Alabama's second-leading scorer Aden Holloway is suspended for the tournament following a felony drug arrest.
Texas Tech is missing JT Toppin, but their overall balance and superior backcourt health favor them in this 4/5 matchup.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
